title: Analysis of Cork-Based Networking

jokes.text: We're like House Vagadosh in the HBO original series Bloodlines of Conquest.
jokes.setup: Character comparing their committee to a fantasy house
jokes.punchline: Reference to obvious Game of Thrones parody with slightly off name
jokes.explanation: Solid pop culture parody with Community's trademark slightly-off reference style

jokes.text: They really get the incest right.
jokes.setup: Discussion of what makes the show good
jokes.punchline: Singling out the incest as a quality element
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with perfect economy - gets big laugh from unexpected casual mention of incest

jokes.text: I don't start watching shows until they're so popular that watching them is no longer a statement.
jokes.setup: Character explaining their TV viewing habits
jokes.punchline: Self-aware description of being a mainstream follower
jokes.explanation: Excellent character-specific humor that perfectly captures a certain type of person's media consumption

jokes.text: So because you're three seasons behind, we have to live in a fantasy world where there's no such thing as dragons?
jokes.punchline: Calling anti-spoiler courtesy a 'fantasy world' when discussing actual fantasy show
jokes.explanation: Classic Abed logical frustration with perfect irony of 'fantasy world' reference

jokes.text: How about I just buy all the Bloodlines of Conquest books, read ahead, and spoil the whole series for you? That's insane. That's something an insane person would do.
jokes.setup: Character threatens to spoil show by reading books
jokes.punchline: Other character calling it insane with perfect matter-of-fact delivery
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with deadpan response, solid but not exceptional

jokes.text: I don't start watching shows until they're so popular that watching them is no longer a statement.
jokes.setup: Character explains their TV viewing philosophy
jokes.punchline: Reveals they're deliberately contrarian about pop culture trends
jokes.explanation: Sharp cultural observation with perfect phrasing. Captures a very specific type of person's relationship with popular culture.

jokes.text: A bulletin board fell down in the cafeteria a couple months ago. Think you can get it hung back up? I'll give it a drive-by.
jokes.punchline: Treating bulletin board hanging like casual criminal activity with 'drive-by'
jokes.explanation: Nice understatement humor with criminal terminology for mundane task

jokes.text: Brince Ironstone's daughter is his mother!
jokes.punchline: Shocked reaction to convoluted incest plot twist
jokes.explanation: Callback to earlier incest joke but fairly predictable Game of Thrones parody

jokes.text: Can you get me a drill, a couple of screws, and a bulletin board? No, I cannot. Okay, I tried. Bye.
jokes.setup: Character asking for tools to hang bulletin board
jokes.punchline: Immediate flat rejection followed by character instantly giving up
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing immediate defeat with perfect comedic timing

jokes.text: Please fill out this form, but don't talk to me. I can't hear.
jokes.punchline: Contradictory instruction to fill out form but not talk while claiming deafness
jokes.explanation: Classic Abed scheme with perfect internal contradiction

jokes.text: Barbagarbagarba garbabarbabarba? That was gibberish. You're good.
jokes.setup: Britta testing if Abed can really read lips
jokes.punchline: Abed immediately identifying her test as gibberish
jokes.explanation: Good setup/payoff with Abed outsmarting Britta's test

jokes.text: Please fill out this form, but don't talk to me. I can't hear.
jokes.punchline: Claims to be deaf but gives verbal instructions, creating logical contradiction
jokes.explanation: Clever Abed scheme - the logical contradiction is immediately apparent but he's committed to the bit. Perfect character integration.

jokes.text: Chang, your last idea was to murder.
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to Chang's previous murderous suggestion
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor with perfect economy and character history integration

jokes.text: Oh, Professor Pushpin! Jerry, Carlo, Crazy Schmidt. I'm Annie.
jokes.punchline: Addressing someone as 'Professor Pushpin' and casually mentioning 'Crazy Schmidt'
jokes.explanation: Annie's over-eager networking with cute nickname and casual crazy reference

jokes.text: That should happen in three to six business months.
jokes.punchline: Absurdly long timeline delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Good bureaucracy satire with deadpan delivery of ridiculous timeline

jokes.text: Lower flag for Reagan's death?
jokes.punchline: Decades-old task still in queue
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist bureaucracy joke with historical reference

jokes.text: Game on, baby.
jokes.setup: Hickey challenges Annie about getting the board hung
jokes.punchline: Annie accepts the challenge with uncharacteristic cockiness
jokes.explanation: Standard challenge acceptance, but works for Annie's character development. Sets up her corruption arc.

jokes.text: Did Mr. Whitney in woodshop make that for you guys?
jokes.setup: Annie notices a wood burnt sign at custodial function
jokes.punchline: Shows she's researched inter-departmental relationships for networking advantage
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Annie's thoroughness in researching the school's social networks for leverage.

jokes.text: Flushes like a DC-10 turbine. Probably exceeds code, but what's wrong with a little friendship between departments?
jokes.punchline: Overly specific praise followed by hint at regulatory violations as 'friendship'
jokes.explanation: Great Annie corruption moment - the specific toilet praise is weird and the regulatory hint shows her learning to play the game.

jokes.text: If I don't break out the fancy sausages, my ass goes in the grinder next January.
jokes.punchline: Meat processing metaphors for workplace politics
jokes.explanation: Solid workplace humor with good metaphor consistency. The meat processing language fits the blue-collar context.

jokes.text: They want their porn unblocked. Then they'll give you your board.
jokes.punchline: Blunt reduction to porn-for-bulletin-board trade
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan reduction of complex corruption to simple terms

jokes.text: Welcome to the labyrinth, kid. Only there ain't no puppets or bisexual rock stars down here.
jokes.setup: Hickey introducing Annie to bureaucratic corruption
jokes.punchline: Contrasting with Labyrinth movie references
jokes.explanation: Great pop culture subversion with Hickey's gruff character voice

jokes.text: That's not what it is to people that lose their pets, people that need rides, people whose bad bands need bad bass players.
jokes.setup: Annie defending importance of bulletin boards
jokes.punchline: Escalating from serious needs to 'bad bands need bad bass players'
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation from heartfelt to absurd with perfect Annie earnestness

jokes.text: To them, every pushpin has the power to change the future.
jokes.setup: Annie's passionate bulletin board defense continues
jokes.punchline: Grandiose language applied to mundane office supplies
jokes.explanation: Perfect Annie idealism - treating minor administrative tools as world-changing. Great character integration.

jokes.text: Bear Down for Midterms.
jokes.punchline: Seemingly nonsensical phrase delivered with confidence
jokes.explanation: Sets up major running gag with Chang's typical confident confusion

jokes.text: Fly on the wall is an expression, but if I want to pitch 'Fly on the Wall for Midterms,' you might ask, 'What do you mean?'
jokes.setup: Character explaining why Chang's idea makes no sense
jokes.punchline: Using equally nonsensical example to prove point
jokes.explanation: Good meta-joke about theme logic with parallel construction

jokes.text: Chang? Are you crying?
jokes.punchline: Surprised recognition of his tears
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment with character vulnerability

jokes.text: Are all Asian men like a joke to you? If it's like a racial thing.
jokes.setup: Chang breaks down about being dismissed
jokes.punchline: Inappropriately brings race into what's clearly about his individual behavior
jokes.explanation: Classic Chang moment - deflecting legitimate criticism by inappropriately playing the race card. Very character-specific.

jokes.text: I don't know if you guys even see me as human anymore. I don't know if it's like a joke to you. Are all Asian men like a joke? If it's like a racial thing.
jokes.punchline: Escalating from personal hurt to race accusations
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable but effective character moment pushing boundaries

jokes.text: I want everything to get through that porn blocker. Everything!
jokes.punchline: Innocent Annie demanding complete porn access
jokes.explanation: Good character corruption moment with Annie's earnest intensity

jokes.text: And then we'll have some cakes, party hats, birthday stuff! / I'm sorry, birthday stuff?
jokes.punchline: Inexplicably includes birthday party elements in midterm dance
jokes.explanation: Good Chang logic failure - his inability to maintain thematic consistency. Duncan's bewildered response is perfect.

jokes.text: You might want to start talking turkey, Waldron, because the Macy's parade is almost over, and grandma's getting drunk.
jokes.punchline: Mixed metaphor connecting business talk to Thanksgiving imagery
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd mixed metaphor that's both confusing and quotable

jokes.text: My power comes from a scarcity of parking, just like your dad's comes from a lack of hugs.
jokes.punchline: Casual burn about Annie's daddy issues
jokes.explanation: Excellent parallel structure with unexpected personal attack

jokes.text: You might want to start talking turkey, Waldron, because the Macy's parade is almost over, and grandma's getting drunk.
jokes.punchline: Mixed metaphor combining Thanksgiving imagery in completely nonsensical way
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd mixed metaphor that somehow works as tough negotiation talk. Very quotable and memorable.

jokes.text: There was plenty of space to park in dinosaur times, but not one single parking department.
jokes.setup: Waldron explains source of parking department power
jokes.punchline: Uses dinosaur times as example of abundance without bureaucracy
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist logic about bureaucratic power. The dinosaur example is perfectly ridiculous yet somehow illustrative.

jokes.text: just like your dad's comes from a lack of hugs.
jokes.punchline: Casual psychological attack suggesting daddy issues
jokes.explanation: Sharp, unexpected personal attack delivered casually. Good economy and timing, though the daddy issues trope is familiar.

jokes.text: well, gobble gobble.
jokes.punchline: Commits to the turkey metaphor literally
jokes.explanation: Good callback that commits to the absurd Thanksgiving metaphor. Simple but effective follow-through.

jokes.text: Too soon, you guys. This is way too soon. After this morning in Wisconsin? Bear breaks loose at a kid's birthday party, mauls a whole bunch of people?
jokes.punchline: Revelation that there was an actual bear attack that morning
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy reveal that recontextualizes innocent bear theme as ghoulish

jokes.text: Bear breaks loose at a kid's birthday party, mauls a whole bunch of people?
jokes.setup: Explaining why the bear theme is inappropriate
jokes.punchline: Reveals their innocent bear theme coincides with tragic bear attack news
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy revelation that recontextualizes everything. The coincidence is both horrible and hilarious.

jokes.text: That where I got the idea. You know how sometimes you hear something and forget you heard it, but you think you came up with...
jokes.setup: Chang realizing where his bear idea came from
jokes.punchline: Admitting he subconsciously plagiarized from tragedy news
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chang moment of accidental insensitivity with dawning realization

jokes.text: I'm not signing this form until Professor Hickey says 'Easy peasy, lemon squeezy.'
jokes.setup: Dean about to sign important form
jokes.punchline: Petty power play over ridiculous phrase
jokes.explanation: Good bureaucratic pettiness with Dean's characteristic childishness

jokes.text: You whored yourself out, kid. You wanted that board so bad that you made every board on campus worthless, and in doing so made the school just a shade dirtier.
jokes.setup: Hickey confronting Annie about her corruption
jokes.punchline: Dramatic moral condemnation over bulletin board
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of stakes with Hickey's moral authority over trivial matter

jokes.text: Oh, man. This got Sorkin-y.
jokes.setup: Intense moral argument about bulletin board
jokes.punchline: Character recognizing the overwrought dialogue style
jokes.explanation: Perfect meta-commentary on the show's own dialogue becoming Aaron Sorkin-esque

jokes.text: Fat Dog for Midterms.
jokes.punchline: Even more nonsensical phrase than Chang's original
jokes.explanation: Escalation of the absurd theme running gag that becomes iconic

jokes.text: Like, don't sweat it, fat dog it. Hang out, relax, like a fat dog. Bah!
jokes.setup: Character explaining the fat dog concept
jokes.punchline: Nonsensical explanation with confident 'Bah!' sound effect
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist follow-up with confident delivery of nonsense

jokes.text: You can look it up on Wikipedia. There's an entry in there.
jokes.punchline: Claiming false Wikipedia validation
jokes.explanation: Solid absurdist commitment with modern reference authority

jokes.text: Fat Dog for Midterms
jokes.setup: Chang presents alternative to bear theme
jokes.punchline: Completely made-up phrase presented as real expression
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist invention. The phrase is complete nonsense but Chang's confidence sells it completely. Highly quotable and memorable.

jokes.text: Just pick an idea and bear down on it. / Um, really poor choice of words, Ben... Too soon.
jokes.punchline: Immediate recognition it's inappropriate given the bear attack news
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the bear attack revelation. The 'too soon' response shows awareness of the dark comedy.

jokes.text: You learned a lesson, and I gave a differently abled person a job.
jokes.setup: Abed defending his fake deaf act
jokes.punchline: Reframing deception as charitable employment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed logic twisting unethical behavior into virtue

jokes.text: ♪ There's a brand-new dance based on an old phrase ♪ ♪ It's called the fat dog, and it will amaze ♪ ♪ You've heard this expression your entire life ♪ ♪ It's not made up, it's not made up ♪
jokes.setup: Song about the fat dog dance
jokes.punchline: Insisting it's a real expression while obviously making it up
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of running gag into full musical number with desperate insistence

jokes.text: ♪ You've heard this expression your entire life / It's not made up, it's not made up ♪
jokes.punchline: Lyrics insist the obviously made-up phrase is real
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of the fat dog concept into full musical number. The defensive lyrics make it funnier.

jokes.text: It's a bear dance!
jokes.setup: Fat dog dance happening at party
jokes.punchline: Someone recognizing it's actually bear-like dancing
jokes.explanation: Nice callback connecting the two theme concepts

jokes.text: Confirmed. Arcadia initiated. What's your clearance level? Top. You really want to activate this, sir? Yeah. Oh! No. No! Can you... Cancel it.
jokes.punchline: Panicking about mysterious activated protocol
jokes.explanation: Decent Chang chaos moment with mysterious sci-fi elements

jokes.text: Confirmed. Arcadia initiated.
jokes.punchline: Treats color selection like military operation with code names
jokes.explanation: Decent escalation of bureaucratic absurdity, though the payoff feels disconnected from the episode's main themes.

tmdb_overview: While Annie and Hickey's fight to get a bulletin board repaired spirals out of control, Abed and Britta go to war over spoilers of his favorite show.
insight: Cork-based networking stumbles at 73.9: character comedy can't sustain absurdist premise alone.
